claim: The Missing Animal Response (MAR) Network was founded by Kat Albrecht in 1997 by applying her professional search-and-rescue (SAR) training methodology to lost pet behavioral patterns, establishing the first formal behavioral profiling framework for lost dog and cat recovery.
stance_target: SAR methodology is directly transferable to lost pet recovery and forms the basis of professional lost pet search strategy
quoted_support: Back in 1997, MAR Network Director Kat Albrecht began to study the behavioral patterns of lost cats and dogs. Due to a background in search-and-rescue (searching for lost people), Kat understood the...

claim: Calling a loose or frightened dog by name triggers a 'flight response' — the dog interprets it as being chased and runs further away — making the search action itself counterproductive to recovery.
stance_target: Searcher behavior affects animal movement and therefore the temporal evolution of the position distribution
quoted_support: Also, calling a pet's name, clapping or whistling can also trigger a panicked 'Oh no – they're looking for me!' flight response in a pet and can be counterproductive.

claim: Scent-tracking dogs used by MARN practitioners narrow the search area by identifying where a lost pet has recently traveled, providing a spatial update that complements eyewitness sightings.
stance_target: Multiple evidence types (scent trail, eyewitness sighting) can be combined to update position estimates
scope_conditions: Professional MAR searches, multiple case testimonials
quoted_support: With the Missing Animal Response Network, & their pet detective, Jamie Bozzi, & search-dog Indy, we were able to narrow down & focus our search efforts on specific locations of where our lost cat had recently been. Watching Indy react to certain areas &, later, hearing from witnesses who had seen our cat, cemented the...

claim: MARN identifies a 'Missing Cat Study' as an original research product by Kat Albrecht studying spatial patterns of lost cats, developed from her SAR background and available at missinganimalresponse.com/missing-cat-study/.
stance_target: Primary research on lost animal spatial behavior exists and is accessible
quoted_support: My background in search-and-rescue work began in 1989 when I set out to train my dog to find lost people and ultimately... WHY THE MISSING CAT STUDY WAS DEVELOPED — By MARN Director Kat Albrecht.
